---

## 🏛️ Community Proposal

> *"A valid proposal must include all five sections. Missing sections will be returned for revision.*
> *This is not gatekeeping. This is quality control."*
>
> — Governance Charter, Article II

---

### 1. Summary
A 1-2 sentence overview of what you are proposing and why it matters.

### 2. Problem Statement
What issue or opportunity does this proposal address? Why does it matter to the Corporate Survivors community?

### 3. Proposed Solution
What change, addition, or action are you requesting? Be as specific as possible.

### 4. Impact Assessment
Who does this affect and how? Consider the community, the project, the token, and any third parties.

### 5. Alternatives Considered
What other options did you evaluate before arriving at this proposal? Why is this approach preferable?

---

### Proposal Category
Select the category that applies (this determines the review timeline per the Governance Charter):

- [ ] **Strategic** — roadmap changes, new partnerships, treasury spending *(7-day community input, 3-day review, 14-day decision deadline)*
- [ ] **Constitutional** — token launch, Charter amendments, major brand changes *(14-day community input, 7-day review, 30-day decision deadline)*

---

> **Before submitting**, please:
> - Review the [Governance Charter](../../governance/) to understand the proposal process
> - Check [GitHub Discussions](https://github.com/CubertCoin/cubertcoin/discussions) to ensure this hasn't already been proposed
> - Ensure all 5 sections above are complete — incomplete proposals will be returned for revision
>
> Proposals missing any of the five required sections will not enter the review process.
